anti_patterns.rationale: Pursuit requires pursuer-quarry asymmetry across the runtime; Forbidden Love requires symmetric mutual desire suppressed by external taboo. Both as A-plot kills both — the engines fight for screen time.
references: Bordwell & Thompson, Film Art (12th ed., McGraw-Hill 2019), Causality chapter

anti_patterns.rationale: Disaster is a structural plot where protagonist agency is overwhelmed by external catastrophe; Love demands a mutual character-transformation arc. Disaster + Love at most yields a B-plot romance; both as A-plots produce a story that satisfies neither doctrine.
references: Polti, The Thirty-Six Dramatic Situations (1916 trans. Lucille Ray), Situation 6

anti_patterns.rationale: Ambition's engine is upward acquisition (climbing toward a peak); Sacrifice's engine is downward giving-up (relinquishing the peak). The same character can do either as primary plot but not simultaneously as the same arc — direction reversal mid-story breaks the line of action.

Enigma demands a withheld-information mystery whose resolution IS the climax; Revenge demands the protagonist already knows the wrong, so the climax is the deed. Same protagonist can do either, but not both as the driving plot — they fight for screen-time and information-budget.

All Sacrificed for Passion arcs the protagonist toward the obsession (deeper into the prior self's drive); Metamorphosis arcs through transformation out of the prior self. The two arcs run in opposite directions; combining them forces a U-turn the audience cannot follow in feature length.
